questions.question: Combined commands consist of forces or agencies from how many allies operating together?
Which of the following is an example of a combined command?
Who establishes unified combatant commands (CCMDs) for the performance of military missions?
options: The Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
The U.S. President, assisted by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff through the Secretary of Defense
Congress, with approval from the Secretary of Defense

questions.question: How often is the Unified Command Plan reviewed and updated?
How many combatant commands (CCMDs) currently exist?
Which of the following is NOT a functional combatant command?
Where is the headquarters of United States Africa Command (USAFRICOM) located?
Where is United States Strategic Command (USSTRATCOM) headquartered?
Once a force is assigned or attached to a unified combatant command, who has the authority to transfer that force?
options: The Secretary of Defense or under special procedures with approval of the U.S. President
The Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff

questions.question: The USAF Total Force is comprised of military and civilian members serving within how many components?
options: Two: RegAF and Air National Guard
Four: RegAF, USAF Reserve, Air National Guard, and Civil Air Patrol
Three: RegAF, USAF Reserve, and Air National Guard
Five: RegAF, USAF Reserve, Air National Guard, Space Force, and Civil Air Patrol

questions.question: Under a classic association in Total Force integration, which component is the host unit?
Command of non-mobilized USAF Reserve units is exercised through whom?
options: The Chief of Staff of the Air Force directly
The Commander, USAF Reserve Command, who is responsible to the Chief of Staff of the Air Force
The Secretary of the Air Force

questions.question: How many Reserve Components of the U.S. Armed Forces are there?
When Air National Guard units are not mobilized or under federal control, who has command jurisdiction?
options: The Chief of Staff of the Air Force
The Secretary of the Air Force
The governor of the state, commonwealth, or possession
The adjutant general of the United States

questions.question: What is the Civil Air Patrol (CAP) designated as when its services are utilized by a federal department or agency?
options: A Reserve Component of the U.S. Armed Forces
The volunteer civilian auxiliary of the USAF
An independent branch of the Department of Defense

questions.question: How many geographic regions is the Civil Air Patrol organized into?
What is the mission of the Air Force Junior Reserve Officer Training Corps (AFJROTC)?
options: To recruit high school students into the USAF
To develop citizens of character dedicated to serving the nation and community
To provide basic military training to high school students
To prepare students for commissioning as Air Force officers

questions.question: The Secretary of the Air Force (SecAF) is appointed by whom?
options: The Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
The U.S. President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate
The Chief of Staff of the Air Force

questions.question: The Chief of Staff of the Air Force (CSAF) is appointed for how many years?
What is a Direct Reporting Unit (DRU) directly subordinate to?
options: The Secretary of the Air Force
The Chief of Staff of the Air Force

questions.question: Which organization is described as the basic unit and building block of the USAF?
Where is Air Combat Command (ACC) headquartered?
Air Force Global Strike Command (AFGSC) is the force provider for how many legs of the nuclear triad?
options: None; AFGSC does not support the nuclear triad

questions.question: What is the minimum adjusted population required for a wing?
A Field Operating Agency (FOA) is a USAF subdivision directly subordinate to which entity?
options: The Chief of Staff of the Air Force

questions.question: What are the specific functions of the USAF regarding strategic capability?
options: Organize, train, and equip air forces for joint operations and strategic missions
Provide ground support exclusively for Army operations
Manage all Department of Defense logistics operations
Conduct naval operations in support of carrier battle groups

questions.question: The Department of the Air Force is composed of what types of forces?
options: Combat air forces and intelligence elements
Air, space, and cyberspace forces, both combat and support

questions.question: Which combatant command is headquartered at MacDill Air Force Base, Florida?
How many geographic combatant commands currently exist?
Where is United States European Command (USEUCOM) headquartered?
Under a 'reverse association' in Total Force integration, which component is the host unit?
What are the three congressionally chartered missions of the Civil Air Patrol?
options: Emergency services, aerospace education, and cadet programs
Search and rescue, intelligence, and recruitment
Air defense, disaster relief, and pilot training

questions.question: The Secretary of the Air Force (SecAF) is responsible for and has authority to conduct all affairs of the Department of the Air Force, including which areas?
options: Only operational command of combat forces
Recruiting, organizing, supplying, equipping, training, servicing, mobilizing, and demobilizing

questions.question: The CSAF presides over the Air Staff, which is composed of how many deputy chiefs of staff?
Where is Air Education and Training Command (AETC) headquartered?
Where is Air Force Materiel Command (AFMC) headquartered?
Where is Air Mobility Command (AMC) headquartered?
A numbered air force (NAF) is a level of command directly under what?
options: The Secretary of the Air Force

questions.question: A group is a tactically self-sufficient unit that is composed of two or more what?
Where is Air Force Global Strike Command (AFGSC) headquartered?
options: Minot Air Force Base, North Dakota
F.E. Warren Air Force Base, Wyoming
